title: It's time to take on woke UK judges and Lords over immigration
meta_description: It's time to take on woke UK judges and Lords over immigration - A ruling class that cannot make people feel safe, secure and respected in their democracy no longer deserves political power, wr...
contents:
A ruling class that cannot make people feel safe, secure and respected in their democracy no longer deserves political power, writes Matthew Goodwin.

By MATTHEW GOODWIN
20:15, Sat, Jul 1, 2023 | UPDATED: 09:47, Sun, Jul 2, 2023

Immigration laws – the American writer Thomas Sowell once said – are the only laws discussed in terms of how to help the people who break them.

I suspect many hardworking Britons thought the same last week when they heard the news that two judges and activist lawyers have blown apart Rishi Sunak’s plan to end illegal migration.

Contrary to the wishes of the British people, The Blob has ruled that the plan to send asylum seekers and illegal migrants to Rwanda is “unlawful” on the grounds it is not safe enough, thereby violating an article in the European Convention on Human Rights.

What does this mean?

It means Rishi Sunak cannot start deporting some of the 100,000-plus illegal migrants who have arrived in Britain since 2018.

It means he will be unable to stop the rising number of small boats crossing the Channel.

It means he will be unable to deter tens of thousands, if not hundreds of thousands, more people from making the perilous crossing.

And, most importantly, it means he will be unable to honour the promise he and his government made to the British people – that they would give them back control of their own borders.

Make no mistake, this ruling is not just about how we should deal with the spiralling number of illegal migrants who are costing the British people billions of pounds each year. It is about something even more important.

It is about democracy.

It is about whether the people who run Britain are in touch with the people who surround them.

It is about whether the people who make the laws in this country still represent the people who have to live with the consequences.

Because the blunt reality today is that if you look at a long line of issues – Brexit, crime, attacks on our history, the erosion of free speech, the gender madness infecting our schools, and the way in which minorities are routinely put ahead of the wider majority – it’s now crystal clear that our democracy is not working as it should.

Consistently, from one issue to the next, Britain is being radically reshaped around the values and voice of a minority of social
liberals and woke progressives.

Almost all of the most important and influential institutions in the country, from the BBC to the civil service, from museums and galleries to the House of Lords, are dominated by a minority who no longer represent the wider majority.

Instead, they spend their days preaching “luxury beliefs” – ideas which bring them social status and applause from other elites, but which impose huge costs on everybody else in society.

One example is their embrace of woke ideology and gender fluidity – eroding the history, identity and culture which the majority cherish.

Another is immigration, where the elite have routinely called for open borders and a massive influx of cheap workers from abroad, while never having to suffer the consequences of their decisions.

They’ve routinely misled the British people.

For more than 20 years, the elite in this country have over-promised and under-delivered.

They told us they would lower immigration, only then to put it on steroids.

They told us they’d Take Back Control of our borders, only then to Completely Lose Control.

They told us they’d wean our economy off the cheap migrant labour undermining British wor- kers, only then to call for even more of it.

They told us they knew who was coming in and out of Britain only then to admit they got the numbers wrong for more than two decades.

And then they told us they’d do whatever it takes to stop the small boats in the Channel, only to refuse to do what it takes to do this.

There’s only one way Rishi Sunak and his team can take back control of Britain’s borders.

They will have to leave the European Convention on Human Rights, reform the Human Rights Act and stare down the activist minority of lawyers, judges, and members of the House of Lords.

That is what they will need to do, not only to regain control of Britain’s borders but to repair our broken democracy and reconnect our distant elites with the masses who surround them.

Because a country that can no longer control its own borders is no longer a serious country.

And a ruling class that cannot make people feel safe, secure and respected in their democracy no longer deserves political power.

Over to you, Rishi Sunak.

You know what you need to do. So for the sake of our democracy get on with it.
